caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5413 rather than 541340 and must remain untouched; it likely underrepresents drafting production roles.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 48.44% residual is the largest judgment and is not a reweighted industry estimate.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Sources demonstrate workflow and software capability, not observed task-level automation rates for 541340.
inputs.pi_dist: S4 is one offshore provider’s offer, not a representative transaction or pricing survey.
inputs.pi_dist: The selected value is a competitive pass-through judgment; it does not assume adoption speed or terminal demand.
inputs.pi_moat: These sources describe work and certification, not a measured pricing-moat survey or legal analysis of every state and client contract.
inputs.pi_moat: Human review may preserve demand but is not counted here as a capture moat.
inputs.t50_years: S3 is a vendor source and gives no NAICS 541340 adoption measurement.
inputs.t50_years: This adoption estimate is independent of target count, owner age, or consolidation.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No source located reports current effective adoption specifically for US 541340 operators or their clients.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Product availability is not equivalent to production-quality adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: One provider’s price menu is not a time series, market share estimate, or causal proof of AI-related deflation.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: The source is 2018 all-industry responding employer businesses, not 541340, the $1–10M EBITDA band, or a current 60+ count.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This general demographic proxy is used only for buyability, not adoption or value capture.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: S7 relies on older, general small-business data and does not establish an industry-specific buyer shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: S5 covers broad architecture and engineering listings and transactions, not a 541340 consolidator census.
inputs.active_consolidators: Absence of a verified public consolidator is weak evidence and should be rechecked in deal sourcing.
inputs.buy_mult: S5 is broad A&E, small-business marketplace data, and seller discretionary earnings rather than 541340 EBITDA transactions.
inputs.buy_mult: There are no verified $1–10M EBITDA drafting-bureau comparables in the reviewed public evidence.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a forward underwriting judgment, not a prediction from observed drafting-services exits.
inputs.exit_mult: S5 uses broad A&E seller-discretionary-earnings multiples, so direct EBITDA comparability is poor.
inputs.pricing_structure: The source is an offshore provider and does not establish the revenue mix or realized pricing of US 541340 firms.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $182,559 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541340); class <5: 2,510 firms, avg revenue/firm $232,175 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $36,335 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 257 firms, avg revenue/firm $967,078 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $151,348 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 124 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,459,411 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $384,898 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 47 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,149,234 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $962,355 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $21,641,667 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,386,921 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,164,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $964,666 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 9

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: S1 and S2 establish a digital production workflow from upstream specifications, while S3 documents currently available workflow automation. Fractions are task judgments for the immutable supplied roles, not observed 541340 automation measurements. The unlisted residual is judged drafting/technician/admin-heavy because the supplied 5413 mix is acknowledged to be unrepresentative of this six-digit industry.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541340 ($693,287,501) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541340 ($1,675,531,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and artifact binding pass, all seven registered sources are accessible, and the cited observations support the thesis direction. The score-driving estimates rely on broad, old, vendor, or single-provider proxies, but those limitations are explicit, the affected inputs are classified as low-confidence estimates with ranges, and no undisclosed contradiction, reversed semantics, or capture/terminal double count was found. The conclusion remains useful only with all authored caveats preserved.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — Sector 54 archive
sources.what_it_supports: Defines 541340 as establishments drawing detailed layouts, plans, and illustrations from engineering and architectural specifications; also distinguishes surveying and mapping activity.

sources.title: Drafters — Occupational Outlook Handbook
sources.what_it_supports: Describes drafters’ CAD/BIM-based work, including converting architect and engineer designs to technical drawings, working from specifications, and the fact that certification is not mandatory.

sources.title: AI in Architecture & Engineering
sources.what_it_supports: Shows current Autodesk A&E AI workflow capabilities, including AutoCAD markup assistance, generative design, and management’s stated implementation limitations and planned AI investment.

sources.title: Pricing — Outsource CAD Conversion / Drafting Services Delhi
sources.what_it_supports: A live provider rate card offering fixed-price and hourly CAD services, including stated $8/hour conversion, sheet-level prices, and architectural/MEP drafting work from India.

sources.title: Architecture & Engineering Business Valuation Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: Reports broad architecture-and-engineering marketplace transaction trends, 2021–25 seller-discretionary-earnings multiples, and the distinction between SDE/cash-flow multiples and revenue multiples.

sources.title: Business Owners' Ages
sources.what_it_supports: Census visualization using 2018 Annual Business Survey data stating that 51% of responding employer-business owners were age 55 or older, with documented coverage limitations.

sources.title: 2.3 Million Small Businesses Nationwide Owned by Aging Boomers Preparing To Retire
sources.what_it_supports: Reports general small-business succession friction, including a statement that one-third of owners over 50 report difficulty finding a buyer; its cited data are not drafting-specific and use older Census inputs.

title: Drafting Services
